executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 99 organizations compromised in a single 24-hour period on April 18, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by both Coinbasecartel and Shinyhunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked by Everest), and a public health unit in Peterborough. The Qilin gang alone claimed 18 Canadian victims including an Eastern Townships school board and multiple technology firms. Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, transportation, energy, and municipal government (Municipality of North Perth).

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware event on April 18, 2026, with 99 confirmed victims across all sectors including critical infrastructure. Major brands Canada Goose and National Money Mart were compromised with data leaks confirmed. Healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, BioPharma Services, multiple clinics), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collège Supérieur De Montréal, Teccart College), municipal government (North Perth), public health (Peterborough), and energy sectors were all hit. No CCCS advisories were identified in this dataset.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was compromised by two separate threat actors (Coinbasecartel and Shinyhunters). Active Green + Ross automotive retailer was hit by Sinobi. Kids & Company childcare services was compromised by Sinobi.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) suffered a confirmed database leak by the Everest ransomware gang. Morison Insurance Brokers was compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting and financial service firms were hit including Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), and SMT Lawyers (Clop). No major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were identified as victims.

report_2w.executive_summary: CCCS has issued an alert for CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, while CISA has added six vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog including critical flaws in Fortinet (CVE-2026-21643), Microsoft, and Adobe products. The ShinyHunters threat actor group leaked 7.54 GB of Rockstar Games data obtained through a third-party vendor breach. Microsoft's April Patch Tuesday addressed two zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-49706 and CVE-2026-32201), and Google patched an actively exploited Chrome zero-day (CVE-2026-5281). Multiple AI agent platforms including Microsoft Copilot and Salesforce Agentforce were found vulnerable to prompt injection attacks allowing data exfiltration.

items.description: Critical SQL Injection vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClient EMS actively exploited in the wild
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Microsoft zero-day vulnerability patched in April Patch Tuesday
Microsoft zero-day vulnerability patched in April Patch Tuesday
Google Chrome zero-day actively exploited in the wild
Adobe Acrobat zero-day exploited for four months in targeted espionage via prototype pollution

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued Advisory AL26-007 on April 7 regarding CVE-2026-35616 impacting Fortinet FortiClientEMS. The Canadian threat landscape report for Q4 2025 highlighted phishing campaigns affecting Canada. No specific incidents targeting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
